name: better-zsh
description: Improved zsh shellscript code editing experience

betterZsh.zshPath.markdownDescription: Path to the zsh binary. Leave empty to use `zsh` from PATH. Set to `off` to never invoke any zsh binary.

languageModelTools.name: zsh_classify
languageModelTools.modelDescription:
Classify a raw zsh token against the bundled static zsh-5.9 reference.

Returns the first matching zsh element from these categories (in classify order):

  - reserved word
  - precommand modifier
  - builtin
  - conditional operator
  - shell parameter
  - process substitution
  - parameter-expansion form
  - parameter-expansion flag
  - subscript flag
  - glob flag
  - glob operator
  - history designator
  - prompt escape
  - ZLE widget
  - option
  - redirection

Each match carries display form, category, and rendered markdown. Returns `{ match: null }` when the token does not name any documented element.

Handles zsh option quirks: case-insensitive matching, underscore stripping, and the NO_* negation convention (without the NOTIFY vs TIFY ambiguity).

No shell execution, no environment access.

raw.description: The raw token as it might appear in zsh source — e.g. "AUTO_CD", "echo", "[[", "<<<", "!$", "<(...)", "NO_NOTIFY". Case and underscores are normalized per category.

languageModelTools.name: zsh_lookup_option
languageModelTools.modelDescription:
Look up a zsh shell option (the names used with `setopt` / `unsetopt`) in the bundled static reference.

Matching is case-insensitive and ignores underscores.

Surfaces `negated: true` when the input was `NO_*` (e.g. `NO_AUTO_CD`) so agents can reason about the state being set, not just the option's identity. Handles the NOTIFY / NO_NOTIFY edge case correctly.

Returns `{ match: null }` when the token is not a documented option.

No shell execution, no environment access.

raw.description: Raw option token as it might appear after `setopt` / `unsetopt`. Example inputs: "AUTO_CD", "autocd", "NO_AUTO_CD", "NOTIFY", "NO_NOTIFY".

languageModelTools.name: zsh_search
languageModelTools.modelDescription:
Search the bundled static zsh-5.9 reference. Fuzzy-matches the query against record ids and display headings across every category.

Omit `query` to list records (optionally filtered by `category`).

Ranking: exact id/display > prefix > fuzzy score.

Results carry `{ category, id, display, score? }` but NOT the rendered markdown body — follow up with `zsh_describe` or `zsh_classify` for the full doc.

`limit` caps response size (default 20, hard max 500). The response also returns `matchesReturned` (== `matches.length`) and `matchesTotal` (pre-truncation total), so `matchesReturned < matchesTotal` signals truncation — raise `limit` or narrow `category`/`query` to see the rest.

Valid `category` values:

  - 'option'
  - 'cond_op'
  - 'builtin'
  - 'precmd'
  - 'shell_param'
  - 'reserved_word'
  - 'redir'
  - 'process_subst'
  - 'param_expn'
  - 'subscript_flag'
  - 'param_flag'
  - 'history'
  - 'glob_op'
  - 'glob_flag'
  - 'prompt_escape'
  - 'zle_widget'

No shell execution, no environment access.

query.description: Optional fuzzy search string matched against ids and display headings. Empty/omitted returns records ordered by corpus iteration, capped by `limit`.

category.description: Optional filter to a single doc category. See command help for valid values. Unknown categories yield an empty match set.

limit.description: Maximum matches to return. Default 20, hard max 500.

languageModelTools.name: zsh_describe
languageModelTools.modelDescription:
Fetch the full record for a known `{ category, id }` from a bundled static zsh reference.

Returns `{ match: { category, id, display, markdown } }` with the rendered markdown body, or `{ match: null }` when the id is not a member of the given category's corpus.

Expects exact canonical ids (typically surfaced by a prior `zsh_search` response). Unlike `zsh_classify` / `zsh_lookup_option` it does NOT apply per-category normalization such as NO_* stripping.

No shell execution, no environment access.

category.description: Doc category — e.g. 'option', 'builtin', 'cond_op', 'reserved_word'. Unknown values yield { match: null }.

id.description: Canonical id within `category` (e.g. 'autocd' for option, 'echo' for builtin). Must be an exact corpus key — usually obtained from `zsh_search`.

scripts.format: biome check --error-on-warnings --write src build.ts tsconfig.json tsconfig.vscode-test.json vitest.config.ts package.json .vscodeignore .vscode-test.mjs scripts
scripts.lint: biome check --error-on-warnings src build.ts tsconfig.json tsconfig.vscode-test.json vitest.config.ts package.json .vscodeignore .vscode-test.mjs scripts
scripts.testINTERACTIVE:electron-bundled: pnpm build && scripts/package-vsix ./better-zsh.vsix && tsc -p tsconfig.vscode-test.json && scripts/testINTERACTIVE-bundled
scripts.vsix: pnpm build && export BZ_SKIP_UPSTREAM=1 && pnpm check && pnpm test && scripts/package-vsix ./better-zsh.vsix
scripts.test:smoke: pnpm build && export BZ_SKIP_UPSTREAM=1 && pnpm check && pnpm test && scripts/package-vsix /tmp/better-zsh-smoke.vsix && rm /tmp/better-zsh-smoke.vsix && echo 'smoke: OK'
scripts.vscode:publish:dry:
pnpm build && export BZ_SKIP_UPSTREAM=1 && pnpm check && pnpm test && pnpm exec vsce ls --no-dependencies && echo '---
(DRY-RUN; ran vsce ls above.)'
scripts.vscode:publish: pnpm build && export BZ_SKIP_UPSTREAM=1 && pnpm check && pnpm test && pnpm exec vsce publish --no-dependencies
scripts.ovsx:publish:dry:
pnpm build && export BZ_SKIP_UPSTREAM=1 && pnpm check && pnpm test && pnpm exec vsce ls --no-dependencies && echo '---
(DRY-RUN for open-vsx; ran vsce ls above.)'
scripts.ovsx:publish: pnpm build && export BZ_SKIP_UPSTREAM=1 && pnpm check && pnpm test && npx -y ovsx -p $OPEN_VSX_TOKEN publish --no-dependencies
